title: primula dryadifolia subsp. chrysophylla
intro: Primula dryadifolia subsp. chrysophylla, also known as Chrysanthemum-leaved Primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Primula dryadifolia subsp. chrysophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant protection. Primula dryadifolia subsp. chrysophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
